meaning: n.(工厂等由于检修,待料等的)停工期
examples: On the production line, downtime has been reduced from 55% to 26%.
Downtime due to worm removal from networks cost close to $450 million.
In order to make the most of our focus and energy, we also need to embrace downtime, or as Newport suggests, "be lazy".
What I used to count on for downtime no longer has so much down in it.
Executives quickly learned the one thing they had to avoid at all costs: downtime.
